courseStructure.title: CSV Fundamentals
topics: What is CSV and common use cases in data engineering
Delimiters (comma, tab, pipe) and header rows
Quoting rules, escaping, and special characters
Line endings (LF vs CRLF) and cross-platform issues
Encoding basics (UTF-8, UTF-16) and BOM issues

courseStructure.title: CSV Quality & Common Problems
topics: Missing values and null handling strategies
Invalid rows, extra columns, and inconsistent schemas
Multi-line fields and how they break parsers
Duplicate headers and column name normalization

courseStructure.title: Schema & Data Types
topics: Inferring data types vs enforcing schema

courseStructure.title: Performance & Storage Considerations
topics: Why CSV is slow for analytics (no column pruning)
Splittable vs non-splittable compression in distributed systems
Small files problem and file sizing guidelines

courseStructure.title: Validation & Best Practices
topics: Row counts, checksum, and reconciliation checks
Data contracts basics (required columns and allowed values)
Creating clean CSV exports for downstream systems

courseStructure.title: JSON Fundamentals
topics: JSON structure: objects, arrays, key-value pairs
Data types in JSON (string, number, boolean, null, object, array)
Common use cases: APIs, logs, events

courseStructure.title: JSON Variants in Data Engineering
topics: JSON Lines / NDJSON (one JSON object per line)
Arrays of objects and normalization patterns

courseStructure.title: Performance & Storage Considerations
topics: Why raw JSON is heavy for analytics (parsing overhead)
When to convert JSON to Parquet/ORC for analytics

courseStructure.title: XML Fundamentals
topics: CDATA sections and special character escaping
Use cases in enterprise systems (feeds, configs, legacy integrations)

courseStructure.title: XML Schema & Validation
topics: Validation concepts and error handling approach

courseStructure.title: Parsing & Transformation
topics: DOM vs SAX vs StAX parsing approaches (high level)
Streaming parsing for large XML files
Flattening XML to tabular structures (mapping approach)

courseStructure.title: Common XML Data Problems
topics: Mixed content and nested repeating groups
Large-file processing patterns and memory considerations

courseStructure.title: Best Practices in Data Pipelines
topics: Convert XML to JSON/Parquet for analytics pipelines

courseStructure.title: Parquet Fundamentals
topics: What is Parquet and why it is used
Column pruning and predicate pushdown concepts
Typical use cases in data lakes and lakehouses

courseStructure.title: Compression & Encoding
topics: Trade-offs: compression ratio vs CPU cost
Impact on scan cost and speed

courseStructure.title: Partitions, File Size & Lake Layout
topics: Small files problem and compaction patterns
Managing partitions in catalog systems (high level)

courseStructure.title: Writing Parquet from ETL Jobs
topics: Validation: row counts, schema checks, sample queries

courseStructure.title: Query Performance Best Practices
topics: Avoiding wide nested columns when not needed
Cost optimization: scan less, store smarter

title: Amazon Redshift
description: Learn Amazon Redshift for analytics: architecture, clusters/serverless, loading data, modeling, performance tuning, security, operations, and building an ELT pipeline with S3 and ETL tools.
prerequisites: SQL fundamentals (JOIN, GROUP BY, window functions). Basic AWS and S3 knowledge helpful.

courseStructure.title: Redshift Fundamentals
topics: What is Amazon Redshift and when to use it
Redshift architecture overview (leader node, compute nodes concept)
Redshift Console, Query Editor v2 basics

courseStructure.title: Networking & Connectivity
topics: IAM roles for Redshift (S3 access patterns)

courseStructure.title: Data Loading & Unloading
topics: Error handling: MAXERROR, error tables, load logs
Unloading data back to S3 (UNLOAD basics)
Incremental loading patterns (date partitions / watermark concept)

courseStructure.title: Table Design & Data Modeling
topics: Sort keys and distribution styles (concepts and use cases)
Choosing distkey/sortkey for common query patterns
Views and materialized views (when to use)

courseStructure.title: Query Performance & Tuning
topics: Common bottlenecks (data distribution, skew, large scans)

courseStructure.title: Security & Governance
topics: IAM authentication and database users basics
Encryption at rest and in transit (overview)
Data masking and least privilege strategy (concept)

courseStructure.title: Operations, Monitoring & Cost
topics: Cost drivers and cost optimization strategies

courseStructure.title: Integrations & ELT Pipeline Patterns
topics: S3 + Redshift pipeline pattern (staging → core → marts)
Athena vs Redshift vs Spectrum (when to use)
Building a reliable load workflow (retries, audit tables concept)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Analytics Warehouse in Redshift
topics: Design a small star schema (facts + dimensions)
Load batch data from S3 using COPY
Run performance checks and apply tuning
Document pipeline and queries in GitHub README

title: Amazon RDS
description: Learn Amazon RDS for managed relational databases: setup, connectivity, backups, HA, read replicas, performance tuning, security, monitoring, and migration patterns used in real projects.
prerequisites: Basic SQL and relational DB concepts (tables, keys, indexes). Basic AWS networking helpful.

courseStructure.title: RDS Fundamentals
topics: What is Amazon RDS and when to use it
RDS engines overview (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQL Server, MariaDB, Oracle)
RDS instance basics (compute, storage, IOPS concepts)
RDS console overview and parameter groups concept

courseStructure.title: Provisioning & Configuration
topics: Choosing instance class and storage type
VPC, subnet groups, and security groups
Public vs private RDS access patterns

courseStructure.title: Backups, Recovery & Maintenance
topics: Maintenance windows and minor version upgrades

courseStructure.title: High Availability & Scaling
topics: Aurora overview for advanced scaling (optional intro)

courseStructure.title: Security & Compliance
topics: Encryption at rest (KMS) and in transit (SSL)
Secrets management basics (AWS Secrets Manager concept)
Network isolation and least privilege access

courseStructure.title: Migration & Data Engineering Use Cases
topics: Common migration patterns (lift-and-shift vs modernization concept)
AWS DMS overview (full load + CDC concept)
Exporting data to S3 for analytics pipelines
RDS as source system vs analytics store (best practice)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Production-Style RDS Setup
topics: Provision an RDS instance in a private subnet
Set CloudWatch alarms and review Performance Insights
Document setup and ops checklist in GitHub README

title: Amazon S3
description: Learn Amazon S3 end-to-end for data engineering and cloud storage: buckets, objects, security, encryption, lifecycle, replication, data lake layouts, performance patterns, event-driven processing, and operations best practices.
prerequisites: Basic cloud concepts helpful but not required.

courseStructure.title: S3 Fundamentals
topics: What is Amazon S3 and common use cases
S3 Console overview and basic operations

courseStructure.title: Access Control & Security
topics: IAM users, roles, and policies for S3 access
ACLs overview (when they appear and why to avoid)
Least privilege patterns for buckets and prefixes
S3 Object Ownership and enforcing bucket-owner control

courseStructure.title: Encryption & Data Protection
topics: SSE-S3 vs SSE-KMS vs SSE-C (high level)
KMS keys basics for S3 (key policy concept)

courseStructure.title: Storage Classes & Lifecycle Management
topics: S3 storage classes overview (Standard, Intelligent-Tiering, IA, One Zone-IA, Glacier tiers)
Choosing the right storage class (cost vs access)
Cost optimization strategies using lifecycle policies

courseStructure.title: Data Lake Design on S3 (Data Engineering Focus)
topics: File naming conventions and folder standards
Small files problem and compaction concepts
Working with CSV/JSON/Parquet on S3 (high level)

courseStructure.title: Performance, Upload Patterns & Reliability
topics: S3 request patterns and prefix distribution
Handling large datasets and operational considerations

courseStructure.title: S3 Event-Driven Integrations
topics: Building ingestion pipelines with S3 events (high level)
Error handling patterns for event-based pipelines

courseStructure.title: Monitoring, Auditing & Governance
topics: CloudTrail events for S3 API activity
Tagging strategy for governance and cost tracking
Data retention and compliance patterns (high level)

courseStructure.title: Hands-On Mini Project — S3 Data Lake Bucket
topics: Create a secure bucket with Block Public Access
Upload sample datasets and apply object tagging
Add a bucket policy for least-privilege access
Enable event notification to trigger a processing step (overview)
Document setup and best practices in GitHub README

title: AWS Glue
description: Learn AWS Glue for batch ETL: Data Catalog, Crawlers, Jobs (Spark), Glue Studio, job parameters, bookmarks, partition handling, error handling, and production-ready ETL patterns.
prerequisites: SQL basics. Basic Python helpful. S3 fundamentals recommended.

courseStructure.title: Glue Overview & When to Use It
topics: What is AWS Glue and common ETL use cases
Glue components: Data Catalog, Crawlers, Jobs, Triggers/Workflows
Glue vs EMR vs Lambda (high level comparison)
IAM roles and permissions for Glue
Typical Glue architecture with S3 + Catalog + Athena/Redshift

courseStructure.title: Glue Data Catalog (Foundation for ETL)
topics: Databases, tables, partitions in Glue Data Catalog
Using Catalog tables in Athena and Spark

courseStructure.title: Crawlers
topics: What crawlers do and how they infer schema
Classifier basics (CSV/JSON) and custom classifiers concept
Handling evolving schemas and partitioned data

courseStructure.title: Glue ETL Jobs (Spark) Basics
topics: Glue job types: Spark, Python Shell (overview)
Reading from S3 and Catalog, writing to S3/Redshift (patterns)

courseStructure.title: Transforms & Data Processing Patterns
topics: Common transforms: select, filter, map, join, groupBy

courseStructure.title: Performance & Cost Optimization
topics: Small files problem and compaction pattern

courseStructure.title: Reliability, Monitoring & Operations
topics: CloudWatch logs and metrics for Glue jobs

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Batch ETL with Glue
topics: Transform to Parquet (silver) with a Glue Spark job
Implement incremental load using partitions or bookmarks
Validate row counts and data quality checks

title: Amazon EMR
description: Learn Amazon EMR for big data batch processing using Spark/Hive/Trino: cluster architecture, scaling, job submission, tuning, security, and cost optimization for production pipelines.
prerequisites: Linux basics, S3 basics, SQL basics. Spark basics helpful.

courseStructure.title: EMR Fundamentals
topics: What is Amazon EMR and when to use it
EMR cluster architecture (master/primary, core, task nodes)
EMR on EC2 vs EMR Serverless (overview)

courseStructure.title: Cluster Setup & Networking
topics: VPC, subnets, security groups for EMR
IAM roles: EMR service role vs EC2 instance profile

courseStructure.title: Spark on EMR (Batch Processing)
topics: Working with partitioned data and Parquet

courseStructure.title: Hive/Trino on EMR (Query Layer)
topics: Hive metastore concept and Glue Catalog integration (overview)
Trino/Presto basics for fast SQL queries
When to use EMR SQL engines vs Athena

courseStructure.title: Scaling & Cost Optimization
topics: Keeping clusters alive vs ephemeral clusters (patterns)

courseStructure.title: Security & Operations
topics: Encryption in transit and at rest (overview)
Troubleshooting failed Spark jobs (logs and common causes)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — EMR Batch Pipeline
topics: Spin up an EMR cluster (or EMR Serverless overview)
Run a Spark job to transform raw data into Parquet
Partition data and write to S3 (silver/gold layers)
Run a SQL query using Hive/Trino to validate outputs
Apply cost controls (auto-terminate / spot strategy concept)
Document setup + tuning notes in GitHub README

title: PySpark for Batch ETL
description: Learn PySpark for batch ETL: DataFrames, transforms, joins, window functions, performance tuning, partitioning, file formats, and building reliable incremental pipelines.
prerequisites: Python basics and SQL basics.

courseStructure.title: PySpark Setup & Core Concepts
topics: What is Spark and why it is used
Lazy evaluation and actions vs transformations

courseStructure.title: DataFrame Transformations
topics: Exploding arrays and flattening nested data (intro)

courseStructure.title: Performance Tuning Essentials
topics: Shuffle basics and reducing shuffle cost
Skew basics and mitigation ideas (high level)
File sizing and small files compaction pattern

courseStructure.title: Incremental Loads & Reliability Patterns
topics: Data validation checks (counts, duplicates, null constraints)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — PySpark ETL Pipeline
topics: Read raw CSV/JSON and standardize schema
Build an incremental load (date partition or watermark concept)
Run validations and publish results summary
Document code + steps in GitHub README

title: Amazon Athena
description: Master Amazon Athena for production analytics on S3: Glue Catalog, partitioning, CTAS, Parquet optimization, Iceberg, security/governance, cost control, and reliable query patterns for data platforms.
prerequisites: Strong SQL (joins, window functions), S3 basics, understanding of partitions and file formats (CSV/JSON/Parquet).

courseStructure.title: Athena Architecture & Setup (Production View)
topics: What Athena is and when to use it vs Redshift/EMR
Workgroups: isolation, query limits, and governance
Query result location and encryption basics

courseStructure.title: S3 Table Design for Athena
topics: Avoiding over-partitioning and small files problem
Compression strategy and file sizing guidelines

courseStructure.title: Table Creation & CTAS (Core Production Skill)
topics: CTAS to convert raw to Parquet (standard pattern)
Partitioned writes and incremental CTAS patterns
Handling nulls, types, and timestamp parsing safely

courseStructure.title: Performance Tuning & Cost Optimization
topics: How Athena pricing works (scan-based) and reducing scanned bytes
Column pruning and predicate pushdown concepts
Using Parquet + compression for scan reduction
Common anti-patterns (SELECT *, unbounded scans, too many small files)

courseStructure.title: Iceberg in Athena (Modern Lakehouse)
topics: Iceberg concepts: snapshots, schema evolution, time travel
Creating and querying Iceberg tables in Athena (patterns)
MERGE/UPSERT style concepts (high level usage patterns)
When Iceberg is better than plain Parquet folders

courseStructure.title: Security, Governance & Access Control
topics: IAM permissions and least privilege for Athena + S3 + Glue
Lake Formation overview for governed access patterns
Audit trails with CloudTrail (query access patterns)
Data masking / row-column level security (high level patterns)

courseStructure.title: Operations, Monitoring & Reliability
topics: Query history, failure debugging, and common errors
Data validation checks (row counts, freshness, duplicates) using SQL

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — S3 Analytics with Athena
topics: Create external tables over raw data on S3
Convert raw to Parquet using CTAS into curated zone
Build partitioned tables and validate pruning
Implement cost controls via workgroups and best practices
Add Iceberg table (optional) and demonstrate time travel concept
Publish README + query examples + architecture diagram

title: AWS Lambda
description: Learn AWS Lambda for production-grade serverless compute: event-driven ETL, integrations, concurrency, reliability patterns, security, observability, deployment automation, and performance optimization.
prerequisites: Python or JavaScript basics, HTTP basics, IAM basics recommended.

courseStructure.title: Lambda Core Concepts (Production Basics)
topics: What Lambda is and best-fit use cases
Timeouts, memory, CPU allocation and tuning

courseStructure.title: Event Sources & Integrations
topics: SQS triggers: batching, visibility timeout, retries
Kinesis triggers: shards, batch size, ordering basics

courseStructure.title: Reliability Patterns (Must for Professional Level)
topics: Retries, backoff, and failure handling patterns
Poison-pill messages and safe handling patterns

courseStructure.title: Concurrency, Scaling & Performance
topics: Cold start basics and mitigation patterns (high level)
Batching and throughput tuning for streams/queues

courseStructure.title: Security & Networking
topics: IAM execution role and least privilege policy patterns
Secure logging and sensitive data handling

courseStructure.title: Observability & Operations
topics: CloudWatch logs and structured logging patterns
Tracing concepts (high level) and debugging flow
Versioning and aliases for safe releases

courseStructure.title: Deployment & Automation
topics: CI/CD approach for Lambda (build, test, deploy concept)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Event-Driven ETL with Lambda
topics: Trigger Lambda on S3 upload and validate input
Transform raw files (CSV/JSON) into curated output (concept/pattern)
Write outputs to S3 with partition naming standard
Add DLQ + retries + idempotency key strategy
Add CloudWatch alarms and operational checklist
Publish README + architecture diagram + test cases

title: AWS Step Functions
description: Learn AWS Step Functions to orchestrate reliable data pipelines: workflows, service integrations, retries, error handling, parallelism, distributed map, monitoring, and production orchestration patterns.
prerequisites: Basic AWS services knowledge (Lambda/SQS/S3). Understanding of async processing is helpful.

courseStructure.title: Step Functions Fundamentals
topics: What Step Functions is and why orchestration matters
Standard vs Express workflows (use cases and trade-offs)
State machine basics and Amazon States Language (ASL) overview

courseStructure.title: Core States & Workflow Building Blocks
topics: Task, Choice, Wait, Pass, Succeed, Fail states
Map state for batch processing patterns

courseStructure.title: Error Handling, Retries & Resilience (Professional Standard)
topics: Retry policies (interval, max attempts, backoff rate)
Timeout patterns and circuit-breaker concepts (high level)

courseStructure.title: Service Integrations (Data Engineering Use Cases)
topics: Glue integration patterns (start job, wait for completion concept)
Athena integration patterns (start query, poll, fetch results concept)

courseStructure.title: Async Patterns & Long-Running Jobs
topics: Callback pattern concept (task token high level)

courseStructure.title: Monitoring, Operations & Cost
topics: Cost drivers (state transitions) and optimization basics
Runbooks for failures, retries, and reprocessing

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Orchestrated Batch ETL Pipeline
topics: Orchestrate an end-to-end batch pipeline (ingest → transform → validate)
Add retries/catch paths for failure handling
Run validation step and write status/audit output
Publish README + state machine diagram + execution screenshots

title: Amazon Kinesis (Data Streams + Firehose + Analytics)
description: Learn Amazon Kinesis for real-time data engineering: Kinesis Data Streams, Firehose delivery, stream processing patterns, ordering, scaling, retries, monitoring, security, and building a production-grade streaming pipeline.
prerequisites: AWS basics, S3 basics, JSON basics. Understanding of async processing and message queues helpful.

courseStructure.title: Kinesis Data Streams Core
topics: Kinesis Data Streams overview and use cases
Stream, shard, partition key, sequence number concepts
Throughput limits and how shards impact scale
Enhanced fan-out vs shared throughput (high level)

courseStructure.title: Producing Events (Producer Side)
topics: Choosing a partition key for ordering and distribution

courseStructure.title: Consuming Events (Consumer Side)
topics: Consumer patterns: Lambda, KCL-based apps, custom consumers
Error handling patterns and poison events

courseStructure.title: Kinesis Data Firehose (Delivery Streams)
topics: Firehose overview and use cases (simple delivery)
Delivery targets: S3, Redshift, OpenSearch (high level)

courseStructure.title: Stream Processing Patterns (Professional Level)
topics: Exactly-once concepts (where it applies and where it doesn’t)
Schema registry concept (enforce schema across producers/consumers)

courseStructure.title: Scaling, Performance & Cost Optimization
topics: Hot shard problem and partition key design fixes
Cost drivers and how to control spend

courseStructure.title: Security, Governance & Compliance
topics: IAM policies for streams and Firehose
KMS encryption for streams and delivery
VPC delivery patterns (when needed, high level)
Secure handling of PII in event payloads (masking concept)
Audit trails and access logging concepts

courseStructure.title: Monitoring, Troubleshooting & Operations
topics: Key metrics: incoming records/bytes, read/write throughput, iterator age
Firehose delivery metrics and failure diagnosis
Disaster recovery concepts (multi-region patterns high level)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Real-Time Data Pipeline with Kinesis
topics: Create a Kinesis stream and define event schema + versioning strategy
Build a producer that publishes events with correct partition key design
Build a consumer (Lambda/KCL pattern) that processes events idempotently
Deliver events to S3 using Firehose (with optional transformation)
Convert stream output to Parquet for analytics (pattern-based)
Monitor lag and set alarms for throttling and delivery failures
Document architecture, scaling plan, and operational steps in GitHub README

title: Google Cloud Storage (GCS)

courseStructure.title: GCS Fundamentals

courseStructure.title: Encryption, Retention & Governance

courseStructure.title: Storage Classes & Lifecycle Rules

courseStructure.title: Data Lake Design on GCS
topics: Small files problem and compaction patterns

courseStructure.title: Performance & Reliability Patterns

courseStructure.title: Events & Integrations (Intro)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Build a GCS Data Lake Bucket
topics: Create secure buckets with IAM least privilege

prerequisites: Python or Java basics, SQL basics, basic GCP concepts (IAM, networking) helpful.

courseStructure.title: Dataflow & Beam Fundamentals

courseStructure.title: Building Batch Pipelines

courseStructure.title: Streaming Pipelines with Pub/Sub

courseStructure.title: Reliability & Error Handling (Professional)

courseStructure.title: Operations, Monitoring & Observability

courseStructure.title: Templates & CI/CD

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Batch + Streaming Pipeline

title: BigQuery
description: Master BigQuery for analytics: SQL, partitioning, clustering, ingestion patterns, optimization, security, governance, and building production-ready data marts and pipelines.
prerequisites: SQL basics. Basic data modeling knowledge helpful.

courseStructure.title: BigQuery Fundamentals
topics: What BigQuery is and typical analytics use cases

courseStructure.title: Ingestion & Loading Patterns
topics: External tables over GCS (when to use)

courseStructure.title: Partitioning & Clustering (Core Professional Skill)
topics: Partitioned tables and partition filter requirement concept

courseStructure.title: Query Performance & Cost Optimization
topics: Query plan and execution details (high level)

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — BigQuery Analytics Mart
topics: Load data from GCS into raw tables

title: Google Cloud Pub/Sub

prerequisites: Basic GCP concepts helpful. Understanding of HTTP and async processing is useful.

courseStructure.title: Pub/Sub Fundamentals

courseStructure.title: Designing Events & Message Contracts
topics: Event payload design (JSON structure best practices)

courseStructure.title: Publishers (Producer Side)

courseStructure.title: Retries, Dead-Lettering & Reliability (Professional)
topics: Dead Letter Topics (DLT) and max delivery attempts

courseStructure.title: Advanced Features (Production Tooling)

topics: IAM roles for Pub/Sub publishers and subscribers

courseStructure.title: Monitoring, Troubleshooting & Operations

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Real-Time Messaging Pipeline

title: Azure Data Factory (ADF)
description: Master Azure Data Factory for batch ETL and orchestration: pipelines, datasets, linked services, Integration Runtime, Copy patterns, parameterization, triggers, error handling, monitoring, and production-ready ingestion design.

courseStructure.title: ADF Fundamentals

courseStructure.title: Copy Activity (Core Skill)

prerequisites: Python basics and SQL fundamentals. Basic understanding of files/Parquet recommended.

courseStructure.title: Databricks Fundamentals

topics: Reusable transforms: modular notebooks and functions

courseStructure.title: Performance & Reliability
topics: Small files issue and compaction concept

courseStructure.title: Jobs, Scheduling & Operations
topics: Cluster policies and cost controls (high level)

courseStructure.title: Security & Secrets
topics: Access control basics: workspace, clusters, jobs

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Databricks Batch ETL Pipeline
topics: Read raw files from ADLS (daily partitions)
Clean and standardize schema + write curated Parquet/Delta
Create a scheduled Databricks Job with retries
Add basic monitoring checklist and runbook
Document architecture + code structure in GitHub README

title: Delta Lake
description: Master Delta Lake for lakehouse batch ETL: ACID transactions, Delta tables, MERGE upserts, schema evolution, time travel, optimization (compaction/Z-ORDER), retention/VACUUM, and production data reliability patterns.
prerequisites: Basic Spark/PySpark and file formats knowledge (Parquet). Familiarity with partitions helpful.

courseStructure.title: Delta Lake Fundamentals
topics: Why Delta Lake: ACID on data lake and reliability benefits
Creating Delta tables and writing data (append/overwrite patterns)

courseStructure.title: Upserts & Incremental Loads (MERGE)
topics: MERGE INTO basics and when to use
Choosing merge keys and deduplication patterns
Idempotent batch loads and rerun safety patterns

courseStructure.title: Schema Evolution & Governance
topics: Handling type changes and safe casting patterns
Column mapping and renamed columns (high level)

courseStructure.title: Performance & Optimization
topics: Small files problem and compaction strategies
OPTIMIZE basics (what it does, when to run)
Partition pruning and file layout impacts

courseStructure.title: Retention, VACUUM & Operations
topics: VACUUM concept and file cleanup basics
Retention policies and safe defaults (high level)
Managing storage growth and housekeeping patterns
Common pitfalls: wrong keys, partition explosion, frequent overwrites
Operational runbook basics for Delta tables

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Delta Upsert Pipeline
topics: Create a Delta table for curated entities
Ingest incremental batches and apply MERGE upserts
Add dedupe keys and idempotent processing logic
Document design + runbook in GitHub README

title: Azure Synapse Analytics
description: Learn Azure Synapse for serving and analytics: workspace, Serverless SQL vs Dedicated SQL, querying lake data, external tables/views, modeling marts, performance basics, security, and operational best practices for production analytics.
prerequisites: Strong SQL basics. Familiarity with ADLS and partitioned data helpful.

courseStructure.title: Synapse Fundamentals
topics: What Synapse is and typical analytics/lakehouse use cases
Core components: SQL pools, Spark pools, pipelines (high level)
Serverless SQL vs Dedicated SQL Pool (when to use)
Serving layer concepts: marts, views, semantic layer (high level)

courseStructure.title: Serverless SQL on Data Lake (Core Skill)
topics: Querying Parquet/CSV in ADLS via Serverless SQL (concepts)
External tables/views patterns for curated datasets
Partition pruning concepts and folder partition strategy
Handling schema drift in files (high level)
Common query patterns for reporting datasets

courseStructure.title: Dedicated SQL Pool (Warehouse) Overview
topics: When dedicated pool makes sense (workload patterns)

courseStructure.title: Data Modeling for BI
topics: Serving patterns for Power BI (high level)

courseStructure.title: Security, Governance & Access
topics: Managed identity access patterns to ADLS (concepts)

courseStructure.title: Operations & Monitoring
topics: Monitoring SQL requests and query performance (high level)
Common issues: permissions, file path errors, partition mistakes
Operational runbooks and incident response checklist

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Synapse Analytics Mart
topics: Query curated data in ADLS via Serverless SQL
Create views/external tables for reporting layer
Design a simple star schema mart (concept + structure)
Apply access control pattern (least privilege concept)
Document architecture + runbook in GitHub README

title: ADLS Gen2 (Azure Data Lake Storage)
description: Learn ADLS Gen2 for modern data lakes: storage accounts, containers, folder standards, security with RBAC/ACLs, networking, lifecycle management, and lake design patterns for batch/streaming analytics.
prerequisites: Basic cloud concepts helpful. Knowing file formats (CSV/JSON/Parquet) is a plus.

courseStructure.title: ADLS Gen2 Fundamentals
topics: What ADLS Gen2 is and where it fits in Azure data engineering
Storage account basics: containers, directories, blobs
Hierarchical namespace (HNS) and why it matters

courseStructure.title: Lake Design Standards (Professional)
topics: Folder naming conventions and dataset partitioning strategy
File formats: CSV vs JSON vs Parquet (when to use)
Small files problem and compaction strategies (concept)

courseStructure.title: Security: RBAC + ACLs
topics: Azure RBAC vs POSIX-style ACLs in ADLS
Setting ACLs at folder level and inheritance concepts
Least privilege patterns for data teams
Managed identities (high level) for accessing ADLS
Secure access patterns for pipelines and notebooks

courseStructure.title: Networking & Secure Access
topics: Storage firewall rules and trusted services
Service endpoints vs private links (high level)
Key Vault concept for secrets (high level)

courseStructure.title: Data Ingestion & Integration
topics: Connecting ADLS with ADF and Databricks (concept)
Landing data safely and validating file arrivals
Basic reconciliation checks (row counts, file counts)

courseStructure.title: Operations, Governance & Cost
topics: Monitoring storage usage and cost drivers

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Build a Lake Layout
topics: Create storage account + containers for zones
Design folder strategy for 2 datasets with partitioning
Upload sample data into raw and curate to Parquet (concept)
Document standards + access model in README

title: Azure Event Hubs
description: Learn Azure Event Hubs for streaming ingestion: event streams, partitions, consumer groups, checkpoints, retention, capture to ADLS, scaling, reliability, and production-ready streaming design patterns.
prerequisites: Basic Azure concepts helpful. Understanding of messaging/streaming basics is useful.

courseStructure.title: Event Hubs Fundamentals
topics: Where Event Hubs fits in Azure streaming architecture
Core concepts: namespace, event hub, partitions, throughput units

courseStructure.title: Event Hubs Capture to ADLS
topics: Choosing formats and folder patterns (high level)
Using captured data as raw streaming zone

courseStructure.title: Security & Networking
topics: Authentication options overview (SAS vs Azure AD at high level)

courseStructure.title: Monitoring & Operations
topics: Key metrics: ingress, egress, throttles, consumer lag
Runbook for replay and incident handling

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Streaming Landing Zone
topics: Create Event Hub with partitions + consumer group
Enable Capture to ADLS and validate folder outputs

title: Azure Stream Analytics
description: Learn Azure Stream Analytics for real-time processing: inputs/outputs, streaming SQL, windowing, late events, reference data joins, error handling, monitoring, and production real-time pipeline patterns.
prerequisites: Basic SQL required. Familiarity with streaming concepts helpful.

courseStructure.title: Stream Analytics Overview
topics: What Stream Analytics is and when to use it
Inputs/outputs overview (Event Hubs, ADLS, Synapse/SQL at high level)
Jobs, streaming units and scaling basics
Common use cases: aggregations, alerts, dashboards

courseStructure.title: Streaming SQL Fundamentals
topics: Derived fields and simple parsing patterns

courseStructure.title: Windows & Time Concepts (Professional)
topics: Tumbling vs hopping vs sliding windows
Event time vs processing time concepts
Choosing window strategies for analytics vs alerting

courseStructure.title: Operations & Monitoring
topics: Cost drivers: streaming units and output choices

courseStructure.title: Mini Project — Real-time Aggregation
topics: Write output to ADLS (curated streaming zone) or SQL (concept)

title: Azure Functions
description: Learn Azure Functions for serverless data engineering automation: triggers/bindings, Event Hub processing, blob triggers, timers, durable workflows (overview), retries, observability, and production deployment patterns.
prerequisites: Basic programming (JavaScript/Python/C#) helpful. Basic Azure concepts recommended.

courseStructure.title: Azure Functions Fundamentals

Local development and configuration patterns (high level)

courseStructure.title: Core Triggers for Data Engineering
topics: Event Hub trigger (stream processing entry point)

courseStructure.title: Bindings & Data Integrations
topics: Calling APIs and handling retries (high level)

topics: Blob trigger: validate incoming files and route to folders

title: Azure Logic Apps
description: Learn Azure Logic Apps for workflow automation and integration: connectors, triggers/actions, approvals, error handling, retries, monitoring, and building production-ready automation flows for data engineering operations.
prerequisites: No-code/low-code friendly. Basic HTTP/API concepts helpful.

courseStructure.title: Logic Apps Fundamentals

courseStructure.title: Integration Patterns for Data Engineering

title: Snowflake Data Platform
description: This course provides hands-on training on the Snowflake Data Cloud, covering data engineering, analytics, performance optimization, security, and administration. You will learn how to build scalable data pipelines, optimize warehouses, manage security, and implement modern data architectures using Snowflake in real-world scenarios.
prerequisites: Basic SQL knowledge and understanding of databases. Familiarity with cloud platforms (AWS/Azure/GCP) is recommended.

courseStructure.title: Introduction to Snowflake & Architecture
topics: Snowflake Architecture: Storage, Compute, Cloud Services
Snowflake UI, Worksheets, and Snowsight Overview

courseStructure.title: Snowflake SQL Fundamentals
topics: Data Types and Semi-Structured Data Types
Querying with SELECT, WHERE, GROUP BY, HAVING
Time Travel Queries and Data Recovery

courseStructure.title: Data Loading & Unloading
topics: File Formats: CSV, JSON, Parquet, Avro, ORC, XML
Unloading Data from Snowflake to Cloud Storage

courseStructure.title: Warehouses, Performance & Cost Optimization
topics: Cost Control: Auto-Suspend, Auto-Resume, Resource Monitors

courseStructure.title: Security & Access Control
topics: Data Encryption (At Rest and In Transit)

courseStructure.title: Data Engineering with Snowflake
topics: Streams and Tasks for Incremental Processing
Orchestration with Tasks and External Tools

courseStructure.title: Snowflake for Analytics & BI
topics: Connecting BI Tools (Power BI, Tableau, Looker)

courseStructure.title: Snowflake Time Travel, Fail-Safe & Data Protection
Snowflake Integration with Cloud Ecosystem
topics: Integrating with Kafka and Streaming Sources

courseStructure.title: Capstone Project (Portfolio Ready)
topics: Design and Build an End-to-End Snowflake Data Platform
Ingest Batch and Streaming Data into Snowflake
Implement Incremental ELT Pipelines using Streams & Tasks
Final Deliverables: GitHub Repo, Architecture Diagram, Demo Walkthrough
